studyGuide.title: Key Points
studyGuide.content:
• An Arithmetic Progression is a list of numbers in which each term is obtained by adding a fixed number to the preceding term.
• The fixed number is called the common difference (d).
• If the first term is 'a' and common difference is 'd', the terms are a, a+d, a+2d...
• AP can be finite or infinite.
• If d > 0, AP is increasing; if d < 0, it is decreasing.

studyGuide.title: Important Formulas
studyGuide.content:
• n-th term (General term): an = a + (n - 1)d
• Sum of first n terms: Sn = n/2 [2a + (n - 1)d]
• Sum using last term (l): Sn = n/2 [a + l]
• Common difference: d = an - a(n-1)
